commentary.text: Rachele takes a link mark on the HFF then deliberately kicks long and quick to the behind line for Neal-Bullen to mark. Nibbler curls in the goal.
Isaac Cumming receives from Neal-Bullen then lets fly from the HFF... across the face and off McAndrews for a behind.
Thilthorpe beats Miller one out to mark a Keays kick to the top of the square, he converts.
Rachele roves and feeds behind a pack to Rankine who misses from 40m on a slight angle.
Rachele had a quiet first half but he begins Q3 with a nice snap off the left from 30m on the flank, after Rankine strips Balta in a tackle.
Isaac Cumming is caught HTB by Lefau at the hotspot, who kicks his first goal.
Campbell roves 20m out, spins and centres to the hotspot for Green, very short kick but it's paid a mark, Green converts and Richmond leads again.
McAuliffe misses off the left after a stoppage near the hotspot.
Adelaide have pinched the lead back, an even contest all day so far.
Broad drops an intercept mark 35m out, Nick Murray stayed down and the crumb falls into his lap, he turns and grubbers through the goal.

#####The cupboard is bare at Tigerland with 17 names on the injury list, all but two of those with an estimated outage of three weeks or more. Tim Taranto returned, at least, but they decided to manage Samuel Grlj and brought in Luke Trainor.
Jordan Dawson rejoins Adelaide, but they lost Taylor Walker and Jordon Butts with Showdown injuries and brought in second-stringers to replace them.

It was a quiet start for the Crows, who were starting Dawson at half back. By half time, stats were just about even, and a slight margin to the Tigers reflected their shots being taken from better spots.

Annoying, thought the Crows were travelling well and a +50pt win today
